title: From “Later Life” by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
VI
We lack, yet cannot fix upon the lack:
Not this, nor that; yet somewhat, certainly.
We see the things we do not yearn to see
Around us: and what see we glancing back?
Lost hopes that leave our hearts upon the rack,
Hopes that were never ours yet seem’d to be,
For which we steer’d on life’s salt stormy sea
meaning: "From “Later Life”" by Christina Rossetti is a sad and love and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 31 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "VI We lack, yet cannot fix upon the lack:...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
